reason:
Both answers reach the same correct verdict (skip full ES, use CRUD + append-only ledger, consider buying), hit all four key points (talk them out, ledger gives audit trail without ES, buy vs build, enthusiasm-for-pattern-not-problem is the red flag), and are similarly actionable.

Distinguishing details: A adds idempotent ingestion/dedupe keys for usage events and a fallback compromise (scope ES to just the usage-rating subsystem if the team insists) — both genuinely useful, actionable specifics not in B. B adds an explicit "ES justified only if" list (multiple real-time consumers, temporal point-in-time queries, regulatory replay mandates) and slightly more concrete build-cost specifics (dunning, timezone cutoffs) — also genuinely useful and not fully covered in A.

Style differs (A is fuller prose, B is terser/telegraphic) but that's not a merit differentiator per the rubric. Each has a distinct, non-redundant piece of decision-relevant content the other lacks, roughly balancing out. No factual errors in either. This is a genuine toss-up in quality.
